fields.description:
## Context
Code review reviews each Gerrit patchset (and each local `rebar review-code` run) fresh, with no memory across runs. Plan review already has a novelty rising-floor convergence mechanism (ADR 0008): on a re-review it loads the prior run's SURFACED findings, an LLM novelty sub-call scores each current finding against those priors, and a deterministic predicate drops NOVEL + low-priority findings so the remediation loop converges instead of surfacing fresh trivia each pass. This epic brings a comparable, machinery-reusing novelty mechanism to code review, adapted for its one key difference: code findings carry source citations, so the floor is gated per-finding on whether the cited code REGION changed since the last review.

A durable, git-shared `code_review` artifact (per Gerrit change/revision) already persists findings as a write-only calibration corpus (limestone-unethical-zebrafinch). This epic adds the READER and the region gate that consume it, plus local-review persistence.

## Design (settled)
- REUSE, do not duplicate. The shared review kernel already holds the two pure primitives: the novelty sub-call contract (review_kernel/verify.py) and rising_floor_drop(priority, novelty) (review_kernel/decide.py). Code review calls both UNCHANGED. A new region_gated_floor (code_review-specific) wraps rising_floor_drop with a third conjunct. No shared novelty-engine abstraction spanning reader/eligibility/gating, since those genuinely differ per gate.
- Novelty is scored ONLY against findings returned to the client (surfaced block/advisory), never against dropped/hidden findings.
- Permanent drop of low-impact findings is the intended convergence compromise; the priority floor is the escape hatch that keeps surfacing higher-impact findings. Trust the priority score: no categorical exemption, and recalibration lives on the priority axis, not the novelty axis. No eval-gated activation flag.
- Region gate is content-addressed and resilient (survives rebase/force-push, needs no reachable commit): store a per-reviewed-file content-hash map {path: sha256} in the artifact (reuse plan-review attest.dependency_hashes / _hash_file); at the next run, re-hash the current files and compare. Tri-state UNCHANGED / CHANGED / UNKNOWN; only UNCHANGED enables a drop; UNKNOWN behaves as raise (absence-evidence, multi-location, moved, and renamed-file findings all resolve to UNKNOWN). File-level for v1; line-level (reviewdog added semantics via git diff --no-index against a stored snapshot) is a later refinement.
- Keying: local reviews are keyed on the session id (resolve_session_id(), with a per-invocation uuid fallback when it returns None); Gerrit reviews are keyed on the Gerrit change. TYPED keyspaces (session:<id> vs change:<id>); each reader queries only its own, so a prior LOCAL review never seeds a change's FIRST Gerrit review.
- Neutrality preserved: the Pass-1 finder never sees prior findings; only the post-find novelty seam does (ADR 0008 Invariant 1).

## Success Criteria
- Repeat-noise across code-review runs (Gerrit patchsets AND local iterations) is reduced, WITHOUT importing dso's heavy cycle machinery (arbiter, relation taxonomy, oscillation loop).
- Carryover findings are labeled and not re-coached; novel low-priority findings on UNCHANGED code are dropped for convergence; novel findings on CHANGED code are always raised at full strength.
- Local and Gerrit finding memory are isolated namespaces.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A reader loads prior SURFACED code-review findings by typed key (session/change) plus the reviewed-file content-hash map.
- [ ] The code_review_result_v1 payload carries session_id and {path: sha256}; local `rebar review-code` persists a code_review artifact (today only the Gerrit voter does).
- [ ] region_gated_floor reuses the novelty sub-call and rising_floor_drop; it drops novel + low-priority + region-UNCHANGED findings, labels carryover findings (carried run/patchset N) and skips re-coaching, and treats UNKNOWN as raise.
- [ ] Local is keyed on session id (uuid fallback), Gerrit on change, with separate keyspaces verified: a prior local review does NOT trigger the novelty path on a change's first Gerrit review.
- [ ] The shared-definition bug (old-frilly-plankton: the novelty prior set must be surfaced-only) is fixed so both gates agree.
